slides.title: The simplest sleep upgrade is a *cooler room*.
slides.sub: Around 65F, your core body temperature can fall on cue. That drop is the signal your body waits for to fall asleep.

slides.title: Sleep starts with cooling
slides.body: To fall asleep, your *core temperature has to drop* about one degree. A cool room makes that shift easier to reach.

slides.title: The thermoregulatory trigger
slides.body: As your body sheds heat through the skin, the falling core temperature *cues sleep onset*. Warmth keeps that switch from flipping.

slides.title: Better slow-wave sleep
slides.body: A cooler bedroom is linked to *more slow-wave sleep*, the deep stage when the body repairs tissue and clears the brain.

slides.title: Aim near 65 degrees
slides.body: Most adults sleep best with the room *between 60 and 67F*. Find your spot in that range and hold it through the night.

slides.title: Help the heat escape
slides.body: Breathable bedding, a warm shower before bed and bare feet *can support the cooling* your body is already trying to do.

slides.title: See how your room shapes *your sleep*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep alongside food, movement and steps, turning small changes like a cooler room into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The easiest way to sleep deeper tonight might be your thermostat. A cool room near 65F helps your core temperature fall, and that drop is what tells your body it is time to sleep. 🌙

Cooler bedrooms are linked to faster sleep onset and more deep, slow-wave sleep. Breathable bedding and a warm pre-bed shower help the heat escape. Save this for tonight. 🔖

Vibe Health turns habits like this into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why a Cooler Bedroom Helps You Sleep Deeper
